prompt: From the INFO field of the following VCF record, calculate the minor allele frequency (MAF). The INFO field contains AF (allele frequency of the ALT allele). Express MAF as a decimal.
context:
#CHROM	POS	ID	REF	ALT	QUAL	FILTER	INFO	FORMAT	IRIS_401	IRIS_402
chr11	8924510	.	C	G	5420	PASS	AF=0.73;AN=600;AC=438;DP=12840	GT:DP:GQ	1/1:22:99	0/1:19:85
correct_answer: MAF=0.27. The AF=0.73 means the ALT allele frequency is 0.73, so the REF allele frequency is 1-0.73=0.27. Since 0.27 < 0.50, the REF allele is the minor allele and MAF=0.27.
rubric: Must state MAF=0.27 (or 0.270). Must show understanding that MAF is the frequency of the less common allele, which in this case is the REF allele.
notes: Tests understanding that AF in VCF is the ALT allele frequency, and MAF is the frequency of the rarer allele -- which can be REF when AF>0.5.
